name: Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga Temple
history: One of the 12 sacred Jyotirlingas, Omkareshwar is situated on an island shaped like the Hindu symbol OM on the Narmada river. The island naturally forms the shape of the sacred syllable OM when viewed from above. The temple has two shrines — Omkareshwar and Amareshwar.
significance: The island temple sits in the middle of the Narmada river and its natural OM shape makes it supremely sacred. A visit here is said to grant moksha. The Narmada parikrama done here is considered equivalent to visiting all holy places.

name: Mamleshwar Temple
history: Mamleshwar is the second shrine of the Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga complex located across the river. Together Omkareshwar and Mamleshwar constitute the single Jyotirlinga of Omkareshwar. Devotees must visit both shrines to complete the Jyotirlinga darshan.
significance: The second part of the Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga. Many devotees are unaware that the complete Jyotirlinga darshan requires visiting both temples. Located on the south bank of the Narmada.

name: Pashupatinath Temple Mandsaur
history: The Pashupatinath temple in Mandsaur houses a rare 8-faced Shivalinga called the Ashta-Mukhi Shivalinga. This is one of the most unique Shivalingas in India and draws pilgrims from across the country. The temple is located on the banks of the Shivna river.
significance: Houses the extremely rare 8-faced Shivalinga. Each face of the linga represents a different form of Lord Shiva. This unique idol is found nowhere else in India making Mandsaur a must-visit for Shiva devotees.

name: Pitambara Peeth
history: Pitambara Peeth is one of the most powerful Shakti shrines in India. The goddess Baglamukhi (Pitambara) is worshipped here and the temple is famous for powerful tantric rituals. Politicians, judges, army officers and celebrities visit before major life events. The goddess is believed to paralyze enemies and grant victory.
significance: One of India most powerful Shakti shrines. The yellow-clad goddess Baglamukhi is believed to grant victory over enemies. Famous for Havan rituals. Highly visited by powerful people before elections and court cases. Extremely rare Dhumavati temple also present here.

name: Shri Ram Raja Temple
history: The only temple in India where Lord Ram is worshipped as a King (Raja) and receives a royal gun salute daily. Ram was brought here as an idol by Queen Ganeshi Bai and when the idol could not be moved to the main temple, a palace was converted into the temple. Ram is worshipped here with full royal protocol.
significance: The ONLY temple in India where Lord Ram is worshipped as a King and receives police salute. The idol is treated as the reigning king of Orchha. An absolutely unique and unforgettable spiritual experience. Chaturbhuj Temple next to it is an architectural masterpiece.

name: Chaturbhuj Temple Orchha
history: Chaturbhuj temple was originally built to house the Ram idol that now resides in Ram Raja temple. The temple has a unique combination of temple and fort architecture. The spire is one of the tallest temple spires in central India and is visible from miles away.
significance: Architecturally stunning — the only temple in India that combines temple architecture with fort design. Has a massive 344-step staircase. The views of Orchha from the top are breathtaking. Originally built for Ram

name: Bijasan Mata Temple
history: Bijasan Mata temple is located on a hill in Indore and is the presiding deity of the city. The goddess is a form of Durga and is extremely popular among locals. The temple sees massive footfall during Navratri when the entire hilltop glows with lamps.
significance: The presiding deity of Indore city. Offers panoramic views of the entire city from the hilltop. During Navratri the temple is decorated with over 10000 lamps creating a breathtaking sight. A must-visit if you are in Indore.

name: Kanch Mandir Indore
history: Kanch Mandir is a stunning Jain temple built entirely of glass mirrors. Every surface — walls, ceiling, pillars, floors — is covered with intricate mirror mosaic work. Built by Seth Hukumchand in the early 20th century, it is one of the most visually stunning temples in India.
significance: One of the most visually spectacular temples in India. Every single surface covered in multicolored glass and mirrors. The effect when light hits is absolutely magical. A photographer dream and a devotee delight.

name: Khajrana Ganesh Temple
history: Khajrana Ganesh is the most visited temple in Indore with thousands of devotees daily. The Ganesha idol here is said to be swayambhu (self-manifested). Ahilyabai Holkar is believed to have discovered the idol and built the temple. The prasad modak distributed here is famous throughout India.
significance: Most visited temple in Indore. The self-manifested Ganesha is believed to fulfill all wishes. The ladoo prasad is legendary. On Wednesdays the queue can be hours long showing the devotion. If you are in Indore Wednesday is the day to visit.

name: Annapurna Temple Indore
history: Annapurna temple in Indore is famous for housing replicas of the Kashi Vishwanath Shivalinga and the original Kashi environment. Built so that elderly devotees who cannot travel to Varanasi can get the same darshan in Indore. The temple complex is beautifully designed.
significance: A gift to devotees who cannot travel to Kashi. Houses authentic replicas of Kashi Vishwanath and the sacred Ganga water. The temple complex is so well designed that it genuinely evokes the feeling of being in Varanasi. Famous for free prasad distribution.

name: Bawangaja Jain Temple
history: Bawangaja houses the tallest Jain idol in the world — a 84-foot monolithic statue of Lord Adinath (Rishabhanatha) carved from a single rock. The statue stands on top of a hill requiring a trek of about 1200 steps. The idol is so large that it can be seen from miles away.
significance: Home to the WORLD TALLEST Jain idol — 84 feet carved from single rock. The trek to reach it is itself a spiritual journey. Views from the top are spectacular. A UNESCO-worthy site that not many people know about.

name: Shri Dwarkadhish Temple Gwalior
history: The Dwarkadhish temple in Gwalior is one of the most beautiful Krishna temples in central India. The temple is located in the heart of Gwalior city and is especially vibrant during Janmashtami when lakhs of devotees gather.
significance: Gwalior main Krishna temple. The Janmashtami celebrations here are legendary with midnight celebrations and elaborate decorations. The idol is beautifully adorned daily.

name: Achaleshwar Mahadev Temple
history: Ancient Shiva temple in the heart of Indore. The temple is known for its unique Shivalinga that is believed to be self-manifested. The temple has been a center of worship for centuries and the area around it has developed into a major market.
significance: One of Indore oldest and most beloved Shiva temples. The self-manifested Shivalinga draws devotees daily. Monday worship here is considered especially auspicious.

name: Bhojpur Temple
history: Bhojpur temple is often called the Somnath of the East. It houses one of the largest Shivalingas in the world — 5.5 feet tall and 18 feet in circumference. Built by the legendary king Raja Bhoj in the 11th century, the temple was never completed — the construction scaffolding marks are still visible on the walls.
significance: The Somnath of the East. Houses one of the world LARGEST Shivalingas — 5.5 feet tall. Built by legendary Raja Bhoj. The unfinished temple is itself a historical mystery — why was it never completed? The engineering of the single massive dome is extraordinary for its time.

name: Birla Mandir Bhopal
history: The Birla Mandir in Bhopal is a stunning white marble temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu and Goddess Lakshmi. Located on Arera Hills it offers panoramic views of the Upper Lake and Bhopal city. The temple is built in the traditional Nagara style using pure white Rajasthani marble.
significance: Stunning white marble temple offering the best views of Bhopal and its famous lakes. The evening aarti with the lake backdrop is breathtaking. A peaceful sanctuary in the middle of a busy city.
